page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 15.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include any local SSD storage or GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance by eliminating resource contention from other tenants. Qualitatively, this configuration offers cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not scale linearly with compute requirements, allowing users to avoid paying for unneeded vCPUs. The instance is suited for small-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, and memory-intensive microservices.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 15.0 GB of RAM. It provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for workloads requiring moderate memory capacity without high compute scaling. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for applications. It is suited for small databases, caching systems, and memory-bound microservices.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 virtual server featuring fifteen gigabytes of memory for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
